vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-905: Command Deck — make Awaiting Merge the canonical final merge gate

plan.title: Command Deck: make Awaiting Merge the canonical final merge gate
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-6

narratives.Problem: After a PR reaches the end of the automated pipeline, the dashboard gives fragmented visibility into whether it's truly ready for the final human merge click. readyForMerge ignores GitHub-native blockers (failing checks, merge conflicts, unresolved conversations), causing false positives on Awaiting Merge. Users must bounce between multiple dashboard panels and GitHub itself.
narratives.Proposal: Three-layer fix: (1) Add real-time GitHub webhook reception via smee.io to get instant blocker updates, (2) Enrich readyForMerge with a typed blockerReasons[] array that captures GitHub-native blockers, (3) Rewrite the Awaiting Merge page and pipeline stepper to surface this data. The pipeline stepper gets a 4th Merge step with CI sub-statuses, queue position, retry history, and live specialist log access.

items.title: Add blockerReasons[] column to review_status SQLite schema

narrative.Action: Add a `blocker_reasons` TEXT column (JSON array) to the `review_status` table in `src/lib/database/schema.ts`. Create a database migration. Define the BlockerReason TypeScript interface in `src/lib/review-status.ts` with types: failing_checks, merge_conflict, unresolved_conversations, changes_requested, draft_pr, not_mergeable. Each entry has type, summary, details?, detectedAt.

subItems.title: review_status table has blocker_reasons TEXT column with migration
BlockerReason interface exported from review-status.ts with all 6 blocker types
getReviewStatus/setReviewStatus serialize/deserialize blockerReasons JSON correctly

narrative.Action: Modify the readyForMerge computation in `src/lib/review-status.ts` (currently lines 172-184) to return false when blockerReasons has entries. Update the ReviewStatus interface to include blockerReasons. Ensure the /api/review/:issueId/status endpoint returns blockerReasons in its response.

subItems.title: readyForMerge returns false when blockerReasons is non-empty, even if review+test passed
/api/review/:issueId/status response includes blockerReasons array

items.title: Add POST /api/webhooks/github route with HMAC-SHA256 verification

narrative.Action: Create `src/dashboard/server/routes/webhooks.ts` with a POST route that: reads the webhook-secret from ~/.panopticon/github-app/webhook-secret, verifies the X-Hub-Signature-256 HMAC header, parses the X-GitHub-Event header, and dispatches to per-event-type handlers. Return 200 on success, 401 on bad signature, 400 on missing headers. Register the route in the server's route composition.

subItems.title: POST /api/webhooks/github validates HMAC-SHA256 signature and rejects invalid payloads with 401
Route dispatches to event-specific handlers based on X-GitHub-Event header
Route gracefully handles missing webhook-secret (logs warning, skips verification when PANOPTICON_DEV_WEBHOOKS=1)

items.title: Implement check_suite and check_run webhook event handlers

narrative.Action: Create `src/lib/webhook-handlers.ts` with handlers for check_suite (completed action) and check_run (completed action). Map the PR from the webhook payload to an issueId via branch name. On check failure: add/update a failing_checks blocker to blockerReasons. On all checks passing: remove the failing_checks blocker. Use existing getPullRequestState pattern for the GitHub API shape.

subItems.title: check_suite completed with failure adds failing_checks blocker to the matched issue's blockerReasons
check_suite completed with success removes failing_checks blocker
Handler maps PR branch to issueId correctly using existing branch naming convention (feature/<issue-id>)

narrative.Action: Add pull_request event handler to webhook-handlers.ts. Handle actions: opened, synchronize, closed, reopened, converted_to_draft, ready_for_review. Detect merge conflicts (mergeable === 'CONFLICTING') and add/remove merge_conflict blocker. Detect draft PRs and add/remove draft_pr blocker. Detect non-mergeable state and add/remove not_mergeable blocker.

subItems.title: Merge conflict detected from PR payload adds merge_conflict blocker
Draft PR adds draft_pr blocker; ready_for_review removes it
Non-mergeable GitHub state surfaces as not_mergeable blocker

items.title: Implement pull_request_review and review_thread webhook handlers

narrative.Action: Add handlers for pull_request_review (submitted action with state changes_requested → add changes_requested blocker; approved → remove it) and pull_request_review_thread (resolved/unresolved actions → manage unresolved_conversations blocker). Handle status event for commit status updates.

subItems.title: Review with changes_requested adds changes_requested blocker
Approved review removes changes_requested blocker
Unresolved review thread adds unresolved_conversations blocker; resolved removes it

items.title: Add smee-client dependency and process management

narrative.Action: Add smee-client as a dependency. Create `src/lib/smee.ts` to manage the smee-client process: start (connects smee channel URL to localhost webhook endpoint), stop, restart on unexpected exit. Smee channel URL stored in ~/.panopticon/github-app/smee-url. If smee-url file doesn't exist, log a warning and skip (webhook reception is optional enhancement).

subItems.title: smee-client process starts and relays GitHub webhooks to POST /api/webhooks/github
Missing smee-url config logs warning and skips without failing pan up
Smee process restarts automatically on unexpected exit

items.title: Integrate smee-client lifecycle into pan up / pan down

narrative.Action: Modify the pan up orchestration to start the smee-client process after the dashboard server. Modify pan down to stop the smee-client process. Add smee process health to pan doctor/pan health output.

subItems.title: pan up starts smee-client process when smee-url is configured
pan down cleanly stops smee-client process
pan health shows smee-client status (running/stopped/not configured)

items.title: Update GitHub App manifest and create migration script for existing installs

narrative.Action: Update `scripts/create-github-app.mjs` to configure webhook events (check_suite, check_run, pull_request, pull_request_review, pull_request_review_thread, status) and set active: true with a smee.io URL during app creation. Create `scripts/update-github-app-webhooks.mjs` for existing installs that updates the GitHub App's webhook config via the API. Store smee URL in ~/.panopticon/github-app/smee-url.

subItems.title: New GitHub App installs (create-github-app.mjs) configure webhook events and smee URL automatically
Migration script (update-github-app-webhooks.mjs) updates existing GitHub App webhook config via API
Smee channel URL persisted to ~/.panopticon/github-app/smee-url

items.title: Add 4th Merge step to pipeline stepper in ReviewPipelineSection

narrative.Action: Add a 4th step to the `steps` array in ReviewPipelineSection.tsx: key='merge', label='Merge'. Map mergeStatus values to step states: pending→pending, queued→running, merging→running, verifying→running, merged→passed, failed→failed. Always show 4 steps (Merge shows as pending/grey until merge phase begins).

subItems.title: Pipeline stepper shows 4 steps: Build Gate → Review → Tests → Merge
Merge step correctly reflects all mergeStatus values (pending/queued/merging/verifying/merged/failed)
Existing 3-step behavior unchanged when merge phase hasn't started (Merge shows as pending)

items.title: Show individual CI check sub-statuses in the Merge step

narrative.Action: When the Merge step is active and CI is running, show individual check statuses (name + pass/fail/running icon) inline below the step. Reuse PR query data from useReviewPrQuery (same data PrDiffTab uses for statusCheckRollup). Reuse the statusColor() mapping pattern from PrDiffTab for consistent visual treatment.

subItems.title: Individual CI checks (build, lint, test, etc.) shown with pass/fail/running icons during merge phase
CI sub-statuses use same data source as PrDiffTab (no duplicate API calls)

items.title: Show mergeRetryCount and mergeNotes in the Merge step

narrative.Action: Display mergeRetryCount as 'Attempt N/3' in the Merge step when retryCount > 0. Show mergeNotes as expandable detail when present (especially on failure). These fields are already returned by /api/review/:issueId/status.

subItems.title: Merge retry count displayed as 'Attempt N/3' when mergeRetryCount > 0
mergeNotes shown as expandable detail section on merge failure

items.title: Show merge queue position in the pipeline stepper

narrative.Action: When queuePosition is returned by /api/review/:issueId/status and is > 0, display 'Position N in merge queue' in the Merge step. Already available in the API response.

subItems.title: Queue position displayed in Merge step when issue is queued behind others

items.title: Add live specialist log link during active merge phase

narrative.Action: When activeSpecialist === 'merge' (returned by review status API), show a 'View live log' link in the Merge step that opens the merge specialist's tmux session in the terminal panel. Use the existing terminal switching mechanism used by specialist log viewing.

subItems.title: Live 'View log' link appears in Merge step when merge specialist is actively running
Clicking link opens the merge specialist's tmux session in the terminal panel

items.title: Rewrite Awaiting Merge filtering to exclude blocked issues

narrative.Action: Modify AwaitingMergePage.tsx to filter out issues with non-empty blockerReasons[]. Issues that have readyForMerge=true but have blockers should not appear in the main 'ready to merge' list. Add a separate 'Blocked' section or visual badge for issues that are close-to-ready but have GitHub-native blockers.

subItems.title: Issues with non-empty blockerReasons do not appear in the ready-to-merge list
Blocked issues shown in a separate section with their specific blocker reason

items.title: Surface exact GitHub-native blockers on Awaiting Merge page

narrative.Action: For each blocked issue on Awaiting Merge, render the blockerReasons inline with appropriate icons and human-readable summaries. Map blocker types to icons: failing_checks→red X, merge_conflict→git merge icon, unresolved_conversations→chat icon, changes_requested→review icon, draft_pr→draft icon, not_mergeable→warning. Show summary text (e.g., '2/5 checks failed'). Expandable detail for full blocker info.

subItems.title: Each blocker type has a distinct icon and human-readable summary
Blocker details are expandable for additional context

items.title: Update frontend ReviewStatusData type to include blockerReasons and new fields

narrative.Action: Update ReviewStatusData interface in queries.ts to include blockerReasons: BlockerReason[]. Add the BlockerReason type to the frontend. Ensure mergeRetryCount and mergeNotes are included in the type (they may already be present in ReviewStatus but not in the frontend type).

subItems.title: ReviewStatusData includes blockerReasons array with proper typing
mergeRetryCount and mergeNotes fields present in frontend types

items.title: Document Panopticon product vision in project docs

narrative.Action: Create a vision document at docs/VISION.md or add a section to existing project docs. Document the phased product vision: (1) Current: single-developer local Electron app, (2) Phase 1: shared instance for a development team, (3) Phase 2: multi-tenant SaaS. Note architectural implications for webhooks, networking, auth, and data isolation.

subItems.title: Vision document exists with the 3-phase product roadmap (local → shared → multi-tenant)
Document notes architectural implications for each phase
